message:
fix(smoke_test): correct shell check logic

The _check_shell() function was incorrectly passing if zsh was simply
installed on the system, even when the user's configured login shell
was something else (like bash).

Before:
  if [[ "$shell" == *"zsh"* ]] || command -v zsh &>/dev/null

This would pass for:
  - User with bash as login shell, but zsh installed on system (WRONG!)

After:
  if [[ "$shell" == *"zsh"* ]]

Now only passes when the user's actual configured login shell contains
"zsh", which is the correct semantics for a smoke test validating that
the installation set up zsh as the default shell.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -90,8 +90,8 @@ _check_user() {
 _check_shell() {
     local shell
     shell=$(getent passwd "$TARGET_USER" 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f7)
-    # Check if configured shell is zsh OR if zsh is installed and linked
-    if [[ "$shell" == *"zsh"* ]] || command -v zsh &>/dev/null; then
+    # Check if configured shell is zsh (the actual login shell, not just that zsh exists)
+    if [[ "$shell" == *"zsh"* ]]; then
         _smoke_pass "Shell: zsh"
         return 0
     else
